  container (centered on screen) instead of the rows div
- justify-end pb-8: tower grows upward from the bottom
- Dynamic --rise-dist per row for foundation-up animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Anchor tower to bottom so words build up from foundation

justify-end pb-8: single word sits at screen bottom, subsequent
words stack upward — visually builds from the ground up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dynamic rise distance: each row rises from proportional depth below base

Bottom row rises 40px, top row rises (N-1)*50+40px — words physically
travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase towerRowIn translateY for more dramatic rise from base

60px travel distance makes each row visibly rise up from below
instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix word placement to build tower from base

Iterate placement loop bottom→top so base rows (wide, appear first in
animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend profanity filter with anatomical and explicit terms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
build(deps): fix minimatch ReDoS vulnerabilities (#95)

## Summary
- Adds `minimatch: ^10.2.3` pnpm override to fix two high-severity ReDoS
vulnerabilities in `minimatch@10.2.1` (transitive dep via `glob`)
- CVE-2026-27903: ReDoS via matchOne() combinatorial backtracking
- CVE-2026-27904: ReDoS via nested *() extglobs
- Resolves dependabot alerts #23 and #24

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm-lock.yaml` shows `minimatch@10.2.4` (>= 10.2.3)
- [x] `npm run build` succeeds
- [ ] Dependabot alerts #23 and #24 auto-close after merge

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

* Updated package configuration with a new dependency override to ensure
consistent behavior across the project.

National NH3 redesign: 7k leads across all 50 states

- NEW ingest.py: reads contacts.xlsx (Luke/Brian/Micah) + DLP join -> data/national_rmp.csv
- REWRITE score.py: tier model (T1-T5 by Locations), national + CA CERS merge, 6,990 total leads
- REWRITE build_html.py: Territory/Tier/State filters, CERS flag badges, contact panels
- UPDATE diff.py: lead_key (epaid) as primary key, tier comparison
- STUB match.py: contact data now comes directly from ingest.py
- UPDATE weekly_update.yml: adds DLP download + ingest step
- Add data/national_rmp.csv and data/dlp_facilities.csv

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(telegram): auto-link admin on bot install (#3644)

* fix(telegram): use link token for account linking instead of broken auto-link

The register route's auto-link used telegramBotId as telegramUserId,
which never matches the actual user's Telegram ID in webhook handlers.
Replace with a link token flow: register returns a linkToken, success
page opens tg://resolve?domain=bot&start=TOKEN, and the /start handler
creates the correct user link with the real Telegram user ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(telegram): use separator-free token format for deep link compatibility

Telegram deep link start parameters only allow A-Za-z0-9_- characters.
The previous format used '.' as separator between payload and signature,
which Telegram silently truncates. Now concatenates without separator and
splits from the end using the fixed HMAC-SHA256 signature length (43 chars).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(telegram): show bot settings for admin even without user link

The GET /api/integrations/telegram endpoint now falls back to checking
telegramInstallations.adminUserId when no user link exists. This lets
the admin see the bot settings and a "Link Account" banner after
installing a bot, even if the deep link account linking hasn't completed.

Also consolidates link token creation by removing the duplicate
createLinkToken in the link route (which still used the broken '.'
separator) and importing from start.ts instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(telegram): use https://t.me/ deep links instead of tg:// protocol

The tg:// protocol doesn't work in all browsers and environments.
Switch to https://t.me/ universal links which reliably open Telegram
on all platforms. Also use window.open instead of window.location.href
to avoid navigating away from the success page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(telegram): auto-link admin account on bot installation

Create a pending user link (telegramUserId='pending') at registration
time instead of generating a link token. When the admin sends their
first message, the pending link is auto-completed with their real
Telegram user ID. This matches the Slack flow where the user link is
created at install time, removing the need for manual /start deep links.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address pr review findings

- Add test for admin fallback path and needsLink field in GET endpoint
- Add integration test for auto-complete pending link on first DM
- Remove dead buildLoginUrl function from telegram shared.ts
- Replace direct DB access in tests with API endpoint verification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(telegram): deduplicate pending link logic and add shared constant

- Add shared constant for pending user id sentinel value
- Extract user link resolution into shared helper
- Remove duplicate auto-complete logic from dm and mention handlers
- Update register route, test helpers, and assertions to use constant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

fix: hide console window during Windows update via PowerShell launcher (#442)

## Summary
- The Windows update batch script (with `ping` delay) was showing a
visible console window for ~3 seconds during update
- Replaced the `cmd.exe` spawn with `powershell.exe -WindowStyle Hidden`
+ `Start-Process -WindowStyle Hidden -Wait` to run the same batch script
invisibly
- The batch script logic is completely unchanged — only the launch
mechanism changes

## Changes
- Added `buildPowershellLauncherArgs()` that generates PowerShell
arguments using `-NoProfile -WindowStyle Hidden` and `Start-Process
-WindowStyle Hidden -Wait` to keep both the PowerShell host and the
child cmd.exe hidden
- Updated both `applyUpdate()` and `applyUpdateOnQuit()` Windows paths
to spawn `powershell.exe` instead of `cmd.exe`
- PowerShell is guaranteed on Windows 10+ (non-removable component), no
deprecation or antivirus risk
- Single quotes in paths are escaped for PowerShell string safety

## Test Plan
- [x] `buildPowershellLauncherArgs` unit tests: -NoProfile, -WindowStyle
Hidden, Start-Process with -Wait, cmd.exe /c invocation, single-quote
escaping
- [x] Existing `buildWindowsUpdateScript` and
`buildWindowsQuitUpdateScript` tests still pass (batch logic unchanged)
- [x] All 25 Windows update tests pass
- [x] Build passes

## Manual Validation
- Install current preview on Windows, trigger an update — verify no
console window appears during the update process
- Verify the installer still runs correctly and the app relaunches after
update

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: 24h day S/R levels (day_high_96 / day_low_96) for Sonnet context

swing_high_20/swing_low_20 only looks 20 candles back (~5hrs on 15M).
Key S/R levels from 6-24hrs ago (e.g. yesterday's swing lows) were
invisible to the AI. This caused the bot to miss an obvious support test.

- Add day_high_96 / day_low_96 in analyze_timeframe() (96-candle = 24h lookback)
- Expose in context_writer.py market_snapshot
- Format in _fmt_indicators() as day_SR=H{high}(+dist)/L{low}(-dist)
  so Sonnet sees the full day's key horizontal S/R alongside BB/fib/pivots

No mechanical rule added — AI uses this to recognise double-test of support,
key horizontal levels, and price at day extremes. Common sense, not overfit.

fix: use Schema.org logo and SVG favicon instead of og:image for badges

og:image is a page preview image, not a logo — it can be a hero banner,
team photo, or product screenshot. Replace it with sources that are
specifically and reliably logo images:

1. Schema.org Organization.logo (JSON-LD) — Google rich-result spec
   requires this to be a clean image on a light/transparent background,
   making it the highest-confidence structured logo source.
   Handles string URLs, ImageObject {url/contentUrl}, @graph arrays,
   and multi-type ["Organization","Corporation"] declarations.

2. SVG favicon (<link type="image/svg+xml">) — vector format, scales
   cleanly at any badge size, always a purpose-built brand mark.

Falls back to apple-touch-icon (existing behaviour) when neither
structured source is present. Google Favicons remains the client-side
data-fallback. The homepage fetch still runs in parallel with job fetches
so there is no extra wall-clock latency.

lib/parse-maps-url.ts for reuse. New /api/admin/restaurants/import
endpoint accepts up to 50 Google Maps URLs with optional per-URL or

per-URL results. Admin UI at /admin/restaurants/import supports both

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sim heading, EKF timing, and thin-ring abort — first complete mission

Three root causes prevented the mower from completing a mowing mission:

1. Dual /gps/imu publishers corrupted EKF heading in sim — heading_to_imu_node
   (real hardware NMEA bridge) published yaw≈0 alongside sim_imu_relay's correct
   heading. Blocked in sim via UnlessCondition(use_sim_time). Also fixed Gazebo
   IMU reporting relative yaw by applying spawn heading offset in sim_imu_relay.

2. EKF at 50Hz couldn't keep up with fast sim clock, causing RPP controller
   "extrapolation into future" aborts. Reduced to 30Hz and added 50ms
   transform_time_offset to both EKFs.

3. Innermost spiral ring (ring 7) was 0.44m wide at the narrow end of the
   trapezoid — too thin to follow. Added minimum-width filter in strip_generator
   that rejects rings narrower than one pass width. Ring count: 7→6.

Also added transit lead-in (20m approach segment aligned with first toolpath leg)
and updated README to reflect CCW spiral toolpath and current sim state.

Result: full 6-ring mowing mission completes successfully (4.3 km, returns to park).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refine: consolidate timestamp skew check into shared helper (#387)

* refine: consolidate timestamp skew check into shared helper

auth.rs and login.rs each had their own inline implementation of the
same timestamp-skew guard — one using saturating_sub/saturating_abs,
the other using abs_diff. Both were correct but violated the one-correct-path
principle: a future security fix to one would silently leave the other
unpatched.

Extract a const fn timestamp_is_stale(now, timestamp) -> bool into
http/mod.rs and replace both inline checks with calls to it. Update
the overflow-safety tests in auth.rs to exercise the shared function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: auto-polish [auto-polish]

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: claude[bot] <41898282+claude[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(chat): Prevent orphaned typing indicator on stream interruption

The isStreaming state was not being reset in several error/cleanup scenarios,
causing the TypingIndicator (blinking cursor) to persist indefinitely.

Root cause: Briefing stream failure and component unmount left isStreaming=true.

Changes:
- Reset streaming state in briefing stream catch block and cleanup function
- Add 60s safety timeout for briefing stream to auto-clear if hung
- Add 120s safety timeout in DialogueMode handleThinking
- Clear streaming state on DialogueMode cleanup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix OSK grid cells retaining bright border after cursor passes

React inline style spreads like ...(cond ? styles : {}) don't unset
properties from a previous render — they just stop setting them,
leaving stale values on the DOM element. Every cell the cursor
passed through kept its bright borderColor from when it was briefly
the cursor position.

Fixed by computing all visual properties (background, borderColor,
color, boxShadow) as explicit values for every cell on every render,
so React always overwrites the previous state rather than leaving
stale properties.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(test): use marker file for fish binary-not-found test on macOS (#1268)

## Problem

The `test_fish_binary_not_found_clear_error::case_1` test fails on macOS
CI because PTY output capture for fish shell returns empty output. The
test asserts on `output.combined.contains("wt: command not found")`, but
the combined output string is empty on macOS.

This is a known macOS PTY behavior — the sibling test
`test_fish_wrapper_binary_not_found_no_infinite_loop` already documents
and works around this:
> "This is reliable even when PTY output capture fails on macOS"

## Solution

Apply the same marker-file pattern used by the sibling test:

1. Write the exit code to a marker file from within the fish script
2. Use the marker file as the **primary check** (exit code = 127)
3. Demote the PTY output assertion to a **secondary check** that only
runs when output is actually captured

This matches the established pattern in the codebase and is robust
across platforms.

## Alternatives considered

- **Retry/increase timeout**: Wouldn't help — the empty output is a
consistent PTY behavior on macOS, not a timing issue
- **Skip on macOS**: Too broad — the test logic is valid, just the
verification method was fragile

## Testing

- Test passes locally on Linux: `cargo test --test integration
test_fish_binary_not_found_clear_error --features
shell-integration-tests`
- Waiting for macOS CI to confirm the fix

run](https://github.com/max-sixty/worktrunk/actions/runs/22697350335)
